channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Is Road To Vostok On PlayStation, Xbox, Or Nintendo Switch? All Console Platforms, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Get ready for another hardcore adventure, as Road to Vostok is ready to enter early access. You’ll be traversing harsh environments whilst dealing with enemies in an ultra-realistic FPS setting, and it’s going to be anything but easy.

However, not all mil-sim fans are on PC, and the Steam release on early access is bound to catch some eyes from the console faithful, but do they stand a chance at playing Road to Vostok?

Luckily for you, Insider Gaming is here to clear the air. Here’s whether you can play Road to Vostok on PlayStation, Xbox, or Nintendo Switch.

Is Road To Vostok On PlayStation?

Sorry, PlayStation fans, it’s not good news: Road to Vostok will not be releasing on the PS5 alongside its PC launch.

In all honesty, though, it’s not all too surprising. The game is only launching in early access, and based on the developer’s description, it could stay that way for two to four years—maybe even more.

  • More Insider Gaming: Road To Vostok Early Access Release Date Countdown

Considering such hardcore titles have always struggled to make their way onto consoles, it makes sense that an early access title like Road to Vostok will be skipping them altogether. But once the game is fully released, it could be an entirely different story, so you’ll have to wait and see.

Is Road To Vostok On Xbox?

It’s the same story as PlayStation, as Road to Vostok will not be released on Xbox Series X|S alongside the PC launch.

If it’s not coming to PlayStation, then it usually means that it’s not coming to Xbox, and the same can be said for Road to Vostok. Again, when the game officially releases, the answer could change, but for the time being, it will remain a PC exclusive.

Is Road To Vostok On Nintendo Switch?

It probably goes without saying, but no, Road to Vostok is not going to be releasing on the Nintendo Switch anytime soon.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is Samson On PlayStation, Xbox, Or Nintendo Switch?

Considering this genre of game struggles to make it onto PlayStation and Xbox, you can only imagine how difficult it’d be to port it onto a handheld Nintendo Switch 2. If you’re a Nintendo fan and Road to Vostok sounds like a bit of you, it might be best to start saving up for a PC, as that’s your best chance at playing it anytime soon.

What platform would you want to play Road to Vostok on? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming platforms guides, check out the following:

  • Pokémon Champions
  • Darwin’s Paradox
  • Mouse: PI For Hire

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Is Samson On PlayStation, Xbox, Or Nintendo Switch? All Console Platforms, Explained
item.content:encoded:
As far as first major releases go for a studio, Liquid Swords is going big with Samson. The action-packed adventure is filled with street brawling and car crashing, and it’s shaping up to be a hell of a release.

However, there does remain some uncertainty regarding which platforms it will be releasing on. Whilst the driving and city of Tyndalston might give you GTA vibes, that doesn’t guarantee that the game will arrive on consoles.

Well, fear not, as Insider Gaming is here to clear up all confusion. Here’s whether you can play Samson on PlayStation, Xbox, or Nintendo Switch.

Is Samson On PlayStation?

Unfortunately, it’s bad news for PlayStation fans, as Samson will not be releasing on the PS5—at least not at launch.

As things stand, Samson will launch exclusively on PC, both on the Epic Games Store and Steam. The reason provided by the developers is that, due to their studio being so small, launching on PC first allows them to deliver the best possible version of the game, without spreading themselves too thin.

  • More Insider Gaming: Samson Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

But don’t worry, as they make it clear that Samson will be arriving on consoles, just at a later date. You can imagine that once the game is released, and in a fully updated state, Liquid Swords’ next focus will be on porting the game onto consoles.

Is Samson On Xbox?

It’s the same story as PlayStation, as Samson will not be released on Xbox Series X|S alongside the PC launch.

That being said, the devs made it clear that console releases were to follow, and it’d be surprising to see Samson miss out on an Xbox port. However, you never know these days, as some games have been known to skip Xbox in the past, but as things stand, it’s fair for Xbox fans to assume that Samson should arrive on the console in the future.

Is Samson On Nintendo Switch?

Rounding off the consoles, it’s a resounding no: Samson is not on either of the Nintendo Switch consoles, but it’s unclear if it ever will be.

  • More Insider Gaming: Every Video Game Releasing In April 2026

The Nintendo Switch 2 is notably more powerful than the original console, with it being able to run the likes of Cyberpunk 2077 with ease. So, it shouldn’t be out of the question that Samson couldn’t appear on the Nintendo Switch 2, but it’s definitely more of a grey area. It’s best to await further details from the developers, rather than get your hopes up in case it’s a disappointing result.

What platform would you want to play Samson on? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming platforms guides, check out the following titles:

  • Pokémon Champions
  • Darwin’s Paradox
  • Mouse: PI For Hire

MOBILE APP

item.title: Sony Announces Acquisition of Cinemersive Labs
item.content:encoded:
Sony has announced that it will acquire machine learning and computer vision company Cinemersive Labs.

We have heard about a lot of acquisitions recently, with Shift Up announcing yesterday that it is acquiring the studio of Resident Evil creator, Unbound. We have been informed of another deal, and this time, Sony is bringing in a new partner to strengthen SIE’s Visual Computing Group.

Sony Interactive Entertainment Brings in Cinemersive Labs to Its Visual Computing Group

A new blog post on the Sony Interactive Entertainment website announces the acquisition of Cinemersive Labs. Sony reveals that this deal is part of their efforts to “new ways to push the boundaries of visual computing and deliver richer, more immersive gameplay experiences.” Cinermersive Labs is a UK-based company that specializes in machine learning and computer vision.

Sony will make Cinemersive Labs join their Visual Computing Group after the acquisition, with the company then contributing to their” broader efforts in advancing state of the art visual computing within games.” The work would include applying machine learning to enhance gameplay visuals, improve rendering techniques, and unlock new levels of visual fidelity for players. 

In other news, Saudi-based Savvy Games is set to acquire Moonton from ByteDance for $6 billion. Additionally, Saudi Arabia’s Electronic Gaming Development Company has acquired a 5% stake in Capcom. What are your thoughts on Sony Interactive Entertainment acquiring Cinemersive Labs? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Raven Software’s co-founder announcing his retirement after 36 years at the helm. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Does Pragmata Have Early Access? Pragmata Demo Status, Explained
item.content:encoded:
If Capcom’s Pragmata has sucked you into its airtight vacuum of hype and you want to play it now, we’re going to dive into its early access status and whether you can play it any other way, if not, AKA a demo.

Pragmata has gone from being an alluring-looking adventure to one of the most secretly anticipated games of 2026. Whilst Pokémon Pokopia, Resident Evil Requiem, and Saros have been the big hitters in the early parts of 2026, Pragmata has been consistently accumulating interest, and it’s got more eyes on it than Pragmata’s research station has AI robots—a lot!

Can You Play Pragmata Early Access?

Capcom is not offering early access for Pragmata, and there is no special or deluxe edition offering any such perk.

Pragmata has different editions, from a basic version to a more expensive Deluxe Edition upgrade, but none of them come with early access. It means your first access to the full game will be with everyone else in April 2026.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pragmata Release Date & Exact Start Times

This close to launch, with everything set in stone, don’t expect any late early access promotions. We can’t rule out another delay, even if the revised launch date has been moved up. However, if anything does change, Insider Gaming will let you know.

Does Pragmata Have A Demo?

On the other hand, there’s good news on the Pragmata demo front as there is one, and it’s available on all major platforms the game’s releasing on.

There’s no early access to play Pragmata in its entirety before launch, but there is a free demo: The PRAGMATA Sketchbook demo.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pragmata’s New York Looks AI-Generated on Purpose, Devs Reveal

Steam says this about the demo:

“The gameplay demo, Sketchbook, lets you try out this combat system that engages both sides of your brain, while you also get to experience what it’s like to traverse the lunar research station.”

It’s not the most substantial demo in the world, and it really is only a snippet of Pragmata. The consensus is that it takes around 15-20 minutes to complete, which is a good deal for a free demo!

It’s free to download on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, PC, and Nintendo Switch 2.

Are you tempted to download the Pragmata Sketchbook demo? If you do play it, let us know what you think: Has it sold you on buying the full game if you weren’t sure already? Message the IG community through our free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord.

For more on Pragmata, check out the game’s director revealing the reason behind frequent delays.

MOBILE APP
